The D.C. Circuit Court of Appeals has dismissed with prejudice a challenge to a U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service rule that makes it illegal to kill or possess a marmot of any kind without a permit.
The lawsuit, filed by the Western Watersheds Project and other environmental groups, challenged the validity of the rule, which prohibits killing or possessing marmots if they are on the Endangered Species Act's endangered species list. Critics of the rule say it is an example of excessive regulation that should be amended.

This has been a pretty exciting week. I got the keys last Friday to our new home, and this morning we closed on the house and the mortgage loan went through!! So now we're the happy owners of a home. I've been very excited about it for a long time, and now it's starting to really feel real. We even have a moving date. We're moving in one weekend, right now!
And, we will be living by the Mississippi in St. Paul! It will be fun to have so much water near us. We've been living in an apartment for 6 months, so we're looking forward to a new and different community to become part of. But, we'll still be in the Twin Cities so we won't be too far away.
So, my days will be busy with moving and cleaning and unpacking and organizing, but it will definitely be exciting.
I'm excited for you that your in a new home! Yea for all new possibilities and change. I know the house is beautiful and I so wish you many happy days in your home.
Moving can be a challenge and exciting all in one.
Thanks, Pam! We're excited about finally living here!
Hooray for new homes.

I was in a similar situation, only it was my partner's mom. She never wanted kids, and at one point she had an "oops" pregnancy. Even though she had an abortion, to this day she resents and blames me for "ruining her life" by forcing her to raise the baby. I was only there because it was my partner's child that I was raising on her behalf.

She's still living in that resentment and blames my partner and I for making her life harder before she got pregnant and for not telling her sooner. 

You're not crazy for feeling this way. Your feelings are valid and I'm sorry for your situation.

This course presents the basic principles of chemistry as they apply to problems frequently encountered in industry. Topics include general chemistry, thermochemistry, kinetics, molecular models for solutions, equilibrium, acid/base chemistry, and electrochemistry. This course also includes practical work, such as data table and diagram preparation and calculation problems. This course is designed to include both lecture as well as a laboratory component.
Prerequisite (or Corequisite): CHEM 010 and CHEM 020 .

Nina Pinturich is a writer, researcher, and media consultant based in Washington, DC. She has also worked as the communications and advocacy manager at the Washington Office on Latin America and as a contributing editor at the Inter-American Dialogue. The United Nations Security Council has repeatedly failed to live up to its own standards for protecting vulnerable populations at risk of genocide or widespread atrocity crimes. We examine the council’s response to the persecution of Rohingya Muslims in Myanmar and find that the council and its Members failed to use the appropriate mechanisms to address the grave crimes that have occurred.
What is a State’s Responsibility in the Face of a Genocide?

There have been some serious developments within our family as of recently. My husband passed away very peacefully on July 6th, 2014. He was at home surrounded by family and friends. He had a good long life of 84 years and had a very good death, surrounded by laughter and love with people he loved and the people he loved loved him. We all know that we never see the really important moments in life, so we always try to focus on the good stuff, on the moments that we do love, but even in that, I think it’s true when they say, when our loved ones go it’s as if the sun was taken from us, not just the one time we lost him but each time we think of him. So we grieve, we feel his absence in our lives, in our hearts and each time we look at him we think that it’s the last time we look at him and we always hope for just one more. And it always leaves us wishing for just one more thing we could have said, one more moment, or time we could have shared together.
I’m not saying I’m sad, I’m not looking back at his life as a negative time, I don’t think of

If you have been watching the news, you may have caught wind of the fact that the federal government is currently in the process of reviewing and creating a new set of rules for medical care and treatment in the United States. This is called the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, or Obamacare for short.
Health care, if you’re looking for coverage as an individual, will become a whole lot different under the new healthcare reform regulations. If you’re an employer looking to cover your workers, things are different as well. And if you’re just looking to have the freedom to choose an insurance plan on your own instead having a single plan or plan that is based off how much money you make, the new federal health care regulations have changed the game completely.
First off are the individual insurance plans. For many individuals, it has become quite the hassle to find coverage. The government is hoping that these regulations will help to ensure that those who need medical care or treatment can get what they desperately need no matter the cost.

I can't speak for the person above you but I know that my parents really didn't trust me in my late teens early 20s when I was going through depression because I had this whole "I hate people that don't fit into my world and make me uncomfortable" thing I carried over from my adolescence. 

I can completely understand why they would be worried when you're going through a rough time and have already had this weird mental thing with the ex. It's not really something that has been proven to actually help anything... It's not helping you and it's kind of an unhealthy attachment to your ex, and not an entirely healthy one either. I mean, I'd prefer my friends to love my partner, wouldn't you.

At www.tastycheese.com we consider the privacy of our visitors to be extremely important. This privacy policy document describes in detail the types of personal information is collected and recorded by www.tastycheese.com and how we use it.
Like many other Web sites, www.tastycheese.com makes use of log files. These files merely logs visitors to the site – usually a standard procedure for hosting companies and a part of hosting services’s analytics. The information inside the log files includes internet protocol (IP) addresses, browser type, Internet Service Provider (ISP), date/time stamp, referring/exit pages, and possibly the number of clicks. This information is used to analyze trends, administer the site, track user’s movement around the site, and gather demographic information. IP addresses, and other such information are not linked to any information that is personally identifiable.
www.tastycheese.com uses cookies to store information about visitors’ preferences, to record user-specific information on which pages the site visitor accesses or visits, and to personalize or customize our web page content based upon visitors’ browser type or other information that the visitor sends via their browser.

It’s probably safe to say that every entrepreneur starts out by trying to do it all by themselves. But, the reality is that the business you have today won’t necessarily resemble the business that you have for the next 20+ years. The world is moving at a breakneck pace, and as your business expands the demands on your time will grow exponentially. It’s a natural step for entrepreneurs to reach a cross-roads where they will ultimately need help with some things.
This may be a bit of a buzz kill, but that’s all fine. It’s better to get this out of the way early as you can be the best at what you do. But that doesn’t mean that you need to hire a team of hundreds of people as your business takes off. I’ve talked before about the importance of hiring slowly to create a culture of collaboration and communication. This is no different for the management of your HR needs. If you can handle a lot on your own, then by all means you can avoid the headache for a while. But most small business owners aren’t this person. The more successful you get the less time you might have to handle these tasks.

I've been in a similar boat before, albeit I wasn't sure what was going on.

First thing that you need to do if you haven't already, is ask the pharmacist or whoever you are talking to what it actually is that you've given to your child. They should be able to explain it and tell you what it should usually feel like or what you should look for. 

Some drugs can make you feel weird, and I think the one that I had used before had something to do with my stomach going really weird, but I don't actually know. So I would have to ask the pharmacist. But whatever it is, they will know, and they will be able to tell you what to do or how to do it. 

You also didn't list how fast you were calling the pharmacy, but I do know sometimes you may get put on hold, and it would be nice if they told you how long to wait before hanging up. If it's in fact a good price for a quality rifle and you can shoot it well then i'd say definitely buy it! As long as it's not something like a junk gun made in a sweatshop with a low-quality and cheap trigger. And definitely ask them to field strip it to double check the internals are all good. But if you have an FFL they can do the transfer which would make it even easier. It's not that much more money than a new lower (which you could end up needing to buy later on if it turns out the original is jank) and it's an even better deal. And when you're ready to build a quality AR that you need an FFL for, you'll have one that you're familiar with.

Annie Lai and her family have been on a mission to improve the lives of their fellow Singaporeans and others all around the globe through the work of Caring Corner, the charity she launched in 2019 to provide food and necessities to those in need.
Lai, 37, believes that every single individual’s actions, no matter how big or small, has the ability to positively impact those around them. A businesswoman for over a decade, Lai, a mother of one and wife to Chinese Malaysian political activist and current PAP Member of Parliament in Jurong GRC Lee Li Lian, is also the co-founder of Lai Siew Wah Co-operation, a small business that has served as a platform for many home-based businesses around the country. For the last three years, she has used the profits from this small business to run Caring Corner, which now aims to expand and do more charitable work.
Caring Corner began as a Facebook group, which saw Lai reach out to close friends to ask them if they had any spare groceries or essential items that they could send to her, which she could then distribute to those in need. Since then, Caring Corner has provided assistance to as many as 25,

Aside from the fact that the show was originally created to be an educational tool for middle school science teachers and had the potential to spark a lot of discussion and debate, I think the way the show was originally conceived was very different than how I actually wanted and wanted to make it. I was a science teacher in the 90’s and didn’t really have a lot to do with the science content of the show when I had the chance to have my say and influence it, so my work on the show has a bit of an interesting origin story, but the science content itself is basically the same: a couple of scientists working as the hosts (I’m one of them). A few things I like to do with the science content in the show include giving a voice to the science community (including myself), and a focus on the science community and its role in society. I like to give the show the feel that it’s a community of people who work together to create and discuss science. I try to present the show as a science community, and I also like to have a couple of guest scientists talk about specific examples of what they’re working on. I like to make it a two way conversation between me and the guest scientists.

When someone says they have been through the worst times of their life, you can almost bet that, for one reason or another they're on to something.
This is the case for Kelsi Smith -- who has battled a number of health issues over the past year -- and was able to pull herself out of that period of darkness by getting a tattoo that meant everything to her and brought her back to a happy point of her life.
While she may not be the most skilled tattoo artist, Kelsi's new tattoo and the story behind it are sure to get you feeling the love.
"I have battled with depression for almost a year and have been through many hardships in the past 6 months -- I had a stroke, double pneumonia, bronchitis and pneumonia, two surgeries and still battling with another surgery." She wrote on her Instagram post.

I have spent a lot of time working with people for whom the concept of being able to see others as having positive attributes is just not something that works for them. For them and I think these traits are often shared, the idea of seeing people as a human being with good traits and a bad side is not only confusing for them but something that can be deeply distressing if it is forced on them at all. I find myself often going through my list of positives and asking a person if they agree to the statement “this person has X positive attribute” where X is typically a quality which would be considered a virtue to me. So far in my experience the only person to do this has the disorder borderline personality disorder and that too is probably a case of the two sides of the brain working as they should.
I have no problem with this type of assessment and the idea in itself is that we have the ability to see a variety of facets of the personality of someone we care for. I believe that in doing this, and in the right context, it can be both therapeutic and validating to the person we are helping. However, the reality of the situation is often the opposite and in that moment of truth often brings about a type of shame or feeling of betrayal.

Crafting the right story is just one facet to crafting a great game. Another key element is how you tell it. I’m talking about the interface — the ways you convey information to your players, the visuals and sounds that provide information and set the tone, the way you craft the flow of game-play.
Some developers pay an awful lot of attention to all these things. Others… not so much.
The game could be really good (the kind that you’d buy again) in spite of a bad interface.
But a bad interface can really hurt the experience and diminish the chance that the player will stick around to make that purchase.
Here’s another way to look at it: It’s the difference between a great film in movie theaters or a movie people loved to stream at home. If the interface is terrible, the experience will be terrible.
As a designer or developer you want everything to blend together seamlessly. This is why the interface needs to be intuitive, logical, and polished.
That’s a lot of ifs, but it’s very much doable.

Police sources said the victim’s family was poor and Subhan used to sexually abuse the girl for nearly a year and a half in her absence at her grandfather’s house in Channabasaveshwara. On learning that his actions were getting exposed, Subhan allegedly ran away from the village.

The purpose of this article is to explain how to get help from the MCCS Team if you are experiencing issues while connecting to Minecraft Education Edition during the Remote Learning phase for the 2020/2021 school year.
The following steps are applicable when trying to connect to a MCCS server hosted by JISC4EDU.
Open your web browser and go to https://connect.nsw.gov.au.
Select Remote Learning.
Select your school using the drop-down menu.
Select the Minecraft Education Edition Portal from the list.
Login to Minecraft Education using your MCCS account.
If the portal is not already open, it will open. If the portal is already open, you will find the connection button in the upper right-hand corner.
If the button for the portal is greyed out, this usually means that JISC4EDU may be experiencing an issue. In this case, you should follow our standard troubleshooting steps.
If the button for the portal is green and your connection fails or there is no port for the server you are playing on, please click here.
If the connection button turns red, it means that the server you are trying to connect to is not online

When my aunt moved up to the big city from a suburb in West Virginia, she said she noticed that people there had less character. She said they all dressed the same and she didn’t feel like she could tell them apart. So she went to a beauty salon and started telling them how she wanted to look, and she bought a wardrobe that would be her new style.
So we’re gonna go with whatever we want. We will go with the people who look at us with that look – the one that says: we love you. We have all been there.
We may not all be able to identify with it, but when we have that look, it can help us to remember that we are all one.
I know that we are all one, but that look can help us to remember that we are all one. People look at us with that look because they think we’re special. But that is one look on us, not on all of us. We are all one, we are all the same. But when we have that look, it can help us to remember that we are all one.
I’m sure most of us have had a look at life that felt like we were some sort of a god.

The New York Institute of Technology is an American private university in New York City. The New York Institute of Technology (NYIT), located in Old Westbury, New York, is a private university. It is the third largest private university in New York State, and is one of only two private, nonprofit universities in New York that are accredited at the university level by the Commission on Higher Education of the Middle States Association of Colleges and Schools. NYIT was founded in 1955 by James Montaigne with a $5.5 million gift (equivalent to $35,000,000 in 2018) from his widow, Anna de Noailles de Montaigne, formerly the first cousin of former President Theodore Roosevelt, in a gift to the newly-created Town of Old Westbury. The gift included the mansion estate and $850,000 of stocks and securities. NYIT enrolls approximately 12,000 students at nine campuses across eight states and in the United Arab Emirates and China.

Here are the three top themes that came out of the research, and some tips of advice to help you maintain strong connections with your employees today, so they don’t feel too isolated at work.
The first theme that came out clearly was the need for flexible working. The second was a need for more communication with colleagues to keep in touch and maintain relationships. And finally, we found that many employees are struggling with a general feeling of isolation at work.

What's happening in New South Wales police raids?
Police are conducting raids across New South Wales on Friday seeking information on a man who allegedly shot two officers following an interstate standoff.
In his only statement to reporters on Thursday, Inspector Kevin Hough said the shootings would be investigated by the Critical Incidents Taskforce.
"The information we have at this stage, and that we share with family, is that our colleagues have been shot," the police commissioner said.
"The suspect is dead, in what I presume is self defence."
Inspector Hough declined to answer questions about whether the suspect had shot at the officers twice before they returned fire.
"I think there's enough information," Mr Hough told reporters.
"It is tragic, but thankfully in the context it's not as tragic as it could have been."
Detectives will examine a silver Ford Territory believed to have been used by the man.
Inspector Hough said the man was believed to be armed and in the car near Liverpool Road, just to the north of the Blacktown Police Station between 5pm and 5:28pm on Thursday.

It's so easy to just "leave" without trying other options if you think they don't work and are just a waste of time. But you'll never know if it would or would not work unless you try, and you don't want to be in that position where the only option left is not going to college at all. Even if it means going to a very low cost college or one that is not well-known and getting your degree at the same time, it's better than not getting one at all. 


When I was in community college and I was planning to go to a university, I decided I wanted to go on an internship and that saved my application. This semester, that's also what I'm going to do, just in a different setting (as opposed to a company). This way, I can not only get paid for doing an internship but also get college credits AND earn a certificate. It's like, the whole purpose of community college is to be a stepping stone to get into university, so you should just take advantage of it, and try to do other things that will get you interested in that field of study rather than quitting, because you never know what might happen in the future and there's always next year

This year’s event is expected to have up to 1,500 booths displaying goods and services by agribusinesses across the country and 100,000 visitors, mainly foreign enterprises, experts and investors. The number was slightly higher than last year when there were 1,200 booths and a total of 98,000 visitors.
The event serves as a bridge to introduce agribusinesses to farmers in the northern region, and create opportunities for farmers to access market information and new knowledge. Exhibits at the event are mainly products and equipment for the agriculture industry, agricultural inputs, and agricultural services.

Avoiding the dreaded "bladder infection"
I am so confused! I thought I was doing everything right. I take a multi-vitamin, eat lots of fruits & veggies, take cranberry & D-mannose. But my UTI keeps coming back! Can somebody give me some simple instructions on what not to do during an active UTI?
Thank you in advance, I'd really appreciate it!
I think you can pretty much cover everything I've learned about UTIs from people who've had them.
This is by no means an exhaustive list, but if I think of anything else, I'll add it.
- If you think you may be getting a UTI, don't take another dose of antibiotics! You should go to the doctor and get tested for UTIs before taking any more antibiotics.

- Antibiotics may cause or worsen the UTIs you are getting.
- The doctor might not test for all bacteria: only the most commonly found.
- The doctor may not do a urine culture, when testing the urine for UTIs.
A urine culture is one of the better ways to identify the bacteria. The bacteria may be resistant to one drug, and sensitive to another drug.

The B.C. government is planning to put forward legislation that would enable municipalities to have greater oversight over industrial developments such as mines and wood pellet processing plants.
According to a news release issued Tuesday (June 22) by the Ministry of Environment, the new legislation will give municipalities greater control over the permitting and management of industrial developments.
“The new legislation will better align the Ministry of Environment’s role as regulator with municipalities as decision makers and planners to achieve better outcomes in our community,” Minister of Environment and Climate Change Strategy George Heyman said in the release.
“By integrating local knowledge, and allowing for more participation by municipalities, this legislation is expected to improve the experience of local stakeholders and residents, and address public concerns while promoting environmental quality and economic prosperity in communities across this province,” the release added.
The release stated that the Bill was drafted to reflect feedback from the public and local government. The Ministry of Environment is inviting feedback from the public by email, which must be sent by the end of this year (July 31, 2020).
The Ministry of Environment said that more information on the Bill can be found online.

I'm glad you've already had a lot of helpful replies, but I'd like to add something too. A lot of us get so caught up in the idea of the perfect relationship that the real people in it get lost. If he doesn't want to talk to you about this, then he's not really putting in the work. 

People don't talk to each other because it's easy to hide our real feelings. You might be having fun or even have fun sometimes, but you're probably lonely.  I used to go out and try to meet people or start conversations with complete strangers while traveling. I just wanted to make a friend that I could be honest with, and it's really a wonderful feeling if you find that person. So, start looking at things differently. See how people respond to you. Make sure they're giving you the same amount of energy that you're giving to them, and don't be afraid to cut them loose if they're not reciprocating that energy. 

You can get through this, but first you have to recognize that you're on a different path right now.

The National Pension Fund (NPF) has issued orders for the acquisition of the Government's stake in eight large state-run corporations including those specialising in railways, ports and cement factories, a business daily reported on Tuesday.
According to the order dated Monday, the NPF will be acquiring 10% stake in Bharati Cements for $5.85m, 5% in the Madras Port for $2.15m, 5% in South Eastern Railway for $7.13m, 3% in Indian Road Network for $1.28m, 2.5% in Gujarat Pipavav for $1.44m, 1.27% in the National Highways Authority of India for $3.65m, 4% in National Cooperative Milk Marketing Federation for $1.28m and 4% in the National Building Construction Corporation for 4.1m dollars, the Financial Express reported.
The NPF had to pay $35 per cent of the face value of the shares in these corporations, according to the order.
With this order, the Government has already reduced its holding in these 13 important state corporations to below 10%.

A new generation of mobile phone apps could help people with severe visual impairment.
The ‘Eyes-free smartphone with virtual reality glasses’ was developed by researchers at Tohoku University’s Graduate School of Engineering and the National Research and Development Agency in Japan.
In a demonstration at the IEEE Sensors 2016 conference held last week, the system allowed users to view a computer screen in a high resolution virtual reality headset, enabling them to see more clearly from further away.
The technology works with both Samsung's Galaxy S7 smartphones and Google’s Daydream View glasses, and a smartphone app turns any smartphone into an Eyes-free controller for virtual reality, with users able to explore a 3D virtual space through voice commands and head movements.
The system will be further tested in Japan and the UK over the next two years, supported by the Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC).

A little known fact about Lent is that it used to last longer than a single 40-day period. It was originally 50 days of fasting and prayer and was celebrated between March 11th until April 10th. The original date for Easter would be just two days shy of Passover, and the whole thing was known as “Great Lent.” It was known as the Passion Week, “Passa” in the language of the early Christians. Passa was the Greek word for “sorrows,” and the Jews had been observing their Passover festival for 3,000 years by the time the first-century Church in Greece got up and running. And their Passover observance and the celebration of Easter had quite a bit in common since they both celebrated the liberation of their people from slavery.
And now consider some biblical reasons we have to celebrate it more than once a year during Holy Week. In fact, we have to remember Passover and Easter in this context every day of the year. Lent is about remembering and commemorating the Passion of Jesus so that we might share in His resurrection. It is a time set aside to help us reconnect with our Christian identity.
When we understand Passover and Easter in these terms, then all

The main purpose of the study was to gain insight into the ways in which a group of Norwegian teachers deal with the challenges of teaching ICT in the secondary schools. A study involving qualitative and quantitative research methods was conducted. The study group consisted of a total of 39 teachers. They all taught ICT to students in lower secondary education in three schools in a town in Norway. The results of the study indicated that the teachers had to learn how to use ICT in the various subjects and subjects. The teachers' experience of ICT in the subject, the teachers' experience of ICT as a tool and the teachers' understanding of the learning environment in the teaching of ICT are used to analyse the way in which they address learning with ICT. To address the research question, a total of seven teachers were chosen for study. The findings show that the teachers felt that technology was a powerful instrument in the teaching environment and that it could support students' learning in many fields. However, they felt that teaching with technology could bring challenges when it comes to the design of teaching and learning as well as in relation to students.

Giving credit for the idea to the show's director (or director's) is pretty standard, even for the most basic jobs. Unless OP's company was the director's or higher, that's just normal. You want to show that you understand your place in the process and it's common courtesy to credit the higher ups.

It also doesn't seem like an insane thing to ask a director "hey can you give me a call in a couple of hours to go over some details on my scene." That's a perfectly reasonable thing for a director to do if you do your job right and make an easy and pain-less experience for them by asking for the extra call beforehand. If you don't get it that's one thing, but just a casual, normal request. Nothing wrong with that at all. There are dozens of people involved in this process, it's not like the guy isn't going to get another chance to call again and get credited (which is still probably the most important part).

A new study suggests that the use of electronic nicotine delivery systems among college students is on the rise. This study by Dr. Susan Farr and colleagues was published July 29, 2019 in the Journal of Adolescent Health.
Previous studies have found that electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) also referred to as e-cigarettes, vape or JUULs, are appealing or popular among college students. Dr Farr and colleagues investigated the prevalence of ENDS use through a nationally representative web-based study of college students.
The researchers surveyed 2,008 American college students in May 2019 via an online survey. The students, mostly women (60%) were enrolled in college across the United States. The students were in grades 9–12, were current or former smokers or had used a tobacco product during the previous 30 days. The average age for the participants was 19 years old. Participants of the study were provided an incentive of $10 for participating in the survey.
More than half (55%) of the sample of college students had heard of ENDS and 16% of the students reported ever having used an ENDS.
Among the 16% of students who had ever used an ENDS were more than twice as likely to have heard of ENDS

If a picture is worth 1,000 words, a digital image worth 1,500,000 words—or more. What could we communicate if every photograph ever made was also a public record, complete with geolocation coordinates? What if it told the story of the lives of the people or thing it shows? It is possible, and you are about to find out.
On-Site Photography is an art course and open-source research project based on the public archive of the American people.
This year (2021) was the centennial anniversary of the end of World War One, and as you all know, there is a very long list of photographers who captured the events and people during the Great War. The first two volumes of The Library of Congress’ American Memory collection provide a great starting place to explore these images, along with the resources provided from this website and links included with the images.
I encourage you to spend time with these images, and think hard about how they would be viewed if instead of photographs they were 1GB+ GIF animations and videos. In the early 1990s, the first models of the Porsche brand appeared in Russia. However, the current Porsche distributor on our market is the French group Automobiles Groupe PS.
Porsche's history can be traced back to 1923, when the Austrian businessman Ferdinand Porsche developed an automobile prototype for the Lohner-Porsche company, which was created that year from the former Austro-Daimler company. The United States Environmental Protection Agency requires the submission of a Water Quality Management Plan (WQMP) by certain facilities in the state of California, including power plants. This WQMP requirement is set forth under Section 301 of the Clean Water Act. The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers is assigned by this statute to accept a copy of the WQMP for the State and then review it and prepare a final report for the U.S. Secretary of the Interior.
A WQMP is required by California law to provide data and information to the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers which will assist with permitting the facility and developing an effective WQMP. Additionally, the final WQMP submitted by the facility and subsequently accepted by the California Department of Public Health is then submitted to the California Statewide Water Quality System to become a publicly available database of water quality information which assists stakeholders in the planning, design and operation of projects, permits and authorizations of wastewater discharges.
These two components of the WQMP process are designed to establish water quality standards for discharges and surface water flows. This provides for compliance with the Clean Water Act and other environmental statutes.

And the composite has low hardness, low thermal conductivity and low tensile strength at ambient temperatures. After high temperature heat treatment, Fe63Mn13Si12Ga13 composite has a high tensile strength, high hardness and low thermal conductivity. Especially its temperature at high temperature heat treatment can reach 450 ℃, and the tensile strength of Fe63Mn13Si12Ga13 can be increased by 25%, the specific heat at high temperature is reduced by 15%. And the wear resistance of Fe63Mn13Si12Ga13 composite is improved by increasing the density. The University of Illinois has filed the application which is used by the University of Illinois to seek non-discrimination protections under Title IX for transgender students. Illinois currently prohibits discrimination on the basis of gender identity and expression – the application is seeking to extend that protection across the state.
On March 22, 2022, Illinois Governor J.B. Pritzker and State Senator Elgie Sims Jr. signed Senate Joint Resolution Constitutional Amendment No. 18, which amends Article I, Section 1 of the Illinois Constitution to add “including, but not limited to, gender identity” as a protected class. The Illinois General Assembly and Governor have indicated that once R18A is effective, local governmental bodies, including public universities, may seek a declaration of the General Assembly that the amendments to the Illinois Constitution prohibit discrimination on the basis of gender identity in all respects. If such a declaration is made, Illinois will become the first state in the nation with broad non-discrimination protections for transgender and gender non-conforming people.

When it comes to high demand jobs, there is always a way to find a skilled worker. There are many options for employers who cannot find the perfect candidates. Whether it’s a long term position or a short term project, the market is not lacking positions.
But the most important part of getting talent to work is being fair and competitive. If you are being overly picky when searching for the talent, there is no guarantee that you will be able to find the right person.
There are a number of issues that arise when you are doing recruitment and need to build a team or department. Here are three that you should consider at some point in your business.
If you hire someone that is too good, you may struggle to keep up with them and they may quit as well. On the other hand, if you hire someone who is average, they might not perform as well and leave in the blink of an eye.
There is a middle ground and the right person for you or your business will always exist in this middle ground.
Your company has the resources, but hiring can take a toll on your staff. Make sure that your recruitment team has enough manpower to handle the workload before you decide on how many people you need to hire.

A lawyer for The Citadel is claiming its student athletes who filed a lawsuit against the university over the cancellation of their spring seasons don’t qualify for monetary relief.
They’re seeking a court order that would restore their athletic credentials, eligibility, and intercollegiate athletics eligibility for the spring 2021 season.
Those attorneys say the athletes are “students in residence of” the university and are owed certain rights as such. The students don’t have “a bona fide prospect for academic credit, or a right to be employed or promoted, and have no contractual relationship with the institution” according to the filing.
Lawyers for the school, however, are fighting back in new court papers claiming the students, who are members of The Citadel’s baseball, basketball, and football teams, are not students under state law because their athletic ability gives them a “special status” and allows them to “squeeze out” a college education.

We're back from a short break here at the blog and here's an issue that I can see being debated for decades: What is the greatest album of all time?
The subject of this debate isn't just about our personal favorites or those with the best song count. We have our own musical tastes, but those are limited by the reality of what's come out in the last three hundred years. So, what is the best album of all time?
You all can debate this among yourselves, but I'd like to throw out the suggestion to each of you: What if you took the best 10 song album of each year, starting in year one?
Of course, any list of albums would likely be based on your personal tastes, which would make it hard to compare it to an existing list of albums. What if you just used songs?
When you look at the charts, most of the big hitters (at least of the last 40 years) have been on the charts for many years. They have been around long enough that you can probably find a 10 song album from every year of the last 40.
You might have a little trouble with the first 20 or 25 years, but that's not a problem for the last twenty years.

I am not the best in the world, because I have failed miserably at some things. But I have many successes for things I have worked through.  

I am married and have children, and have been married many years, and have never been alone. I have a great job, that I love, that allows me to do a lot of cool things, like play with my kids, take my own vacation, play my own games and have my own stuff. There are many things I have to get done but it is a good life.

You are not alone, and it is okay.  It is okay to accept that things are hard.  It is okay to have doubts and ask yourself if things are worth it. It is okay to take a break for a few days, a few weeks, even a year.  And it is okay to realize that this is the life you are meant to live. Not only that, but to be happy with what you have been given.

Motorway work: how are local authorities getting on?
The government has yet to specify how much funding could be made available to local authorities under the public services agreements, which aim to support councils with their transport needs. It is not known whether the PSAs will be subject to the same review.

In the UK, at a school that doesn’t have a swimming pool, the Year 7s do a unit on water safety to prepare the children for a residential trip they do every year - they have to swim 100m in shallow warm water, 10m in cold water, jump into deep water without touching the bottom, have to find and tie the correct kind of knot for their rope (so they don’t tie any knots they can’t undo on the trip), swim underwater holding their breath, rescue each other if necessary at varying depths, and do a number of other water safety skills including rescue drills. 

I think it’s so important for our students to feel comfortable in water. In the north of the uk we’re always told to be cautious about taking young children to the beach or even in a paddling pool or paddling bath. 

I agree with your post that children should feel confident in the water, and not be afraid of it. But I also think it’s useful (and necessary for some people) to be able to swim, and to be confident in a public space when they are in or near water. This is especially true for children who live near the ocean where there is significant risk of rip tides

This study examined the relationship between social participation and health related quality of life (HRQoL) among older adults from five provinces of China (N=2494), and used the Chinese version of EQ-5D to measure HRQoL. We found that HRQoL, as determined by the Chinese EQ-5D, is strongly related to social participation and social support. Compared to those who had not experienced a fall, those who fell experienced greater HRQoL impairments. Compared to those who were satisfied with social contact, those who were not satisfied with social contact experienced less HRQoL. Compared to those from urban areas, those from rural areas experienced less HRQoL. However, this was not true for women. We also found that age was associated with HRQoL; those from older age groups (60-75 and 75+ years) experienced greater HRQoL impairments than those from younger age groups (50-59 and 60-69). We found that urbanization was associated with HRQoL only among men, such that men in urban areas and from middle income groups experienced the greatest HRQoL impairments. These findings have significant policy implications; they highlight the importance of older women's health and well being in policies aimed at improving social wellbeing and public

An investigation by a US House subcommittee finds that several state and federal agencies under-reported the number of people who are killed by gun violence by hundreds or thousands annually.
A House Oversight Committee investigation found that law enforcement officials “substantially under-report” the number of people killed by gun violence as crimes involving multiple victims.
At least two-thirds (64 percent) of the cases were under-reported by law enforcement agencies. For the remaining 32 percent, there was insufficient evidence to confirm the report.
One way underreporting is thought to be affecting data collection is by ignoring non-fatal shootings and homicides and only counting homicides that do not use a firearm.
Some of the underreporting was due to the lack of a reporting policy.
A recent bill introduced in Congress would require the collection of statistics in all states that would allow the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to make accurate estimates on gun-related deaths nationwide and across state lines.
The data would improve the ability of law enforcement to find suspects and the public to understand gun violence in their communities.
“It’s unacceptable that gun-related violence deaths are occurring at such a high rate without a clear picture of our country’s epidemic,” said Senator Tammy Duck

The Turbosmart BCS Boost Control Solenoid is the new generation of the classic BCS solenoid design with a more durable brass construction that is ideal for high boost applications. The BCS Solenoid has a compact design that is designed to fit between the turbo and throttle body. With its innovative Billet aluminium body and brass fitting, the BCS Solenoid offers a better seal as well as better flow than the original unit. The BCS Solenoid also eliminates wastegate rattle usually associated with the standard valve and can be installed in a matter of minutes.
The Turbosmart BCS Boost Solenoid can easily be calibrated by changing the internal spring in the valve by removing 4 screws, loosening the fitting and then sliding the rod to the length required. As a result of this, the BCS Solenoid is fully configurable between 0.5 -1.5bar (5 -35psi) which will adjust the boost control based on airflow changes. The BCS Solenoid is also easily configurable during installation by setting the position on the bracket and screw

One day, a man was walking past a bakery when he noticed a box labeled "Ocean Breezes". Intrigued, he picked up the box and opened it. The air flowed out, and instead of ocean breezes, he breathed in a sweet floral scent. When he looked down, to his shock and surprise, he found himself with an erection.
That evening, he went to the bakery again and bought the entire stock of Ocean Breezes. The next day, he went back and bought up the entire supply of Ocean Breezes, and the next day, and the next...
He sold all his possessions, and used his money to buy a house on the beach. He went to the police, explaining that as soon as he stepped outside of his house, his erection was triggered. The police agreed to put a watch on him for awhile...
When the officer came inside of the house, the man quickly turned on an Ocean Breezes scent air freshener and went over to his computer to write a letter to the owner of Ocean Breezes, asking him to send him the bill to his house so he could sue for copyright infringement.

Females are more likely to be prescribed an antipsychotic if they are hospitalized for psychosis or other mental health conditions.
Hospitalizations of both male and female patients with serious psychiatric distress and psychosis have increased in number since 1999, but the increase in female hospitalizations was significantly higher. According to a study published online in the journal Schizophrenia Bulletin, women may also be given significantly different, and possibly more dangerous, treatment.
For the study, the team led by Renee S. Rubin, M.D., M.P.H., a former NIMH-funded Postdoctoral Fellow working at the University of Michigan (UM) and currently a Medical Officer at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention’s (CDC’s) National Center for HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, STD and TB Prevention, analyzed national hospital discharge data to observe trends in hospitalizations for psychotic disorders, specifically schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. They analyzed a sample of 11.9 million adults and children hospitalized with psychotic disorders between 1999 and 2010.

Phoenix is the capital and most populous city in Arizona, with 1,620,352 residents as of 2018.
Since 2014, Phoenix has been one of the fastest-growing large cities in the United States.
Phoenix, nicknamed the Valley of The Sun, has more than 460,000 acres of park land. Residents and visitors enjoy more than 600 miles of hiking and biking trails. Phoenix averages over 300 days of sunshine a year, making this a great city for outdoor activities and sports. The Phoenix area also encompasses four other incorporated cities, Tempe, Mesa, Scottsdale and Glendale. These cities have thriving art communities and cultural attractions. Many Phoenix residents attend sporting events at Phoenix Suns Arena home of the NBA basketball Suns as well as the NFL’s Arizona Cardinals at University of Phoenix Stadium. Other attractions include the Phoenix Zoo, Desert Botanical Garden and Heard Museum.

A group of more than 50 organizations and academic institutions are urging the Biden administration to address barriers to the federal vaccine mandate for healthcare workers. The letter, submitted to the U.S. Department of Labor, suggests the implementation of several strategies to better facilitate exemptions to vaccine mandates among healthcare workers. The letter is signed by leaders within medical groups, state health systems, healthcare associations, and academic medical centers.
The signatories recognize challenges that exist among healthcare workers due to vaccine mandates. These include concerns about limited access to effective treatments and the inability to access certain accommodations.
The implementation of new guidance will facilitate the granting of accommodations to these concerns. This includes implementing accommodations for healthcare workers who are infected with COVID-19 due to their occupation. Accommodations may include alternative work assignments, telework, paid leave, and/or alternative duty assignments.
It is not uncommon for exemptions from vaccination to be granted based on medical and religious status. If healthcare workers or their caregivers request an exemption in these circumstances, it can be granted in accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, the Rehabilitation Act, Title VII of the Civil Rights Act (religion/belief), and Executive Order 14042 and 14051 for religious/belief exemptions.

It’s a great question, and one we hear often, because the answer is a bit complicated and depends on what you are looking for. What does it mean to “grow” any given type of fruit tree? Is it simply a matter of the type of tree’s age, or rather a measure of its size relative to, say, a mature shrub? Or is it a combination of age and growth rate? Let’s consider each of these approaches in turn.
Grapefruit trees can be very small or quite large, but there’s no question about one thing: grapefruit trees are large, and if you’re looking for a fruit tree that will grow as fast as possible, grapefruit trees are probably what you want.
Grapefruit trees are grown from seeds. Seeds come in a few different varieties: some are green, while others have yellow or purple skins, and some are even red! As a general rule, however, the larger the seed the more likely it is to produce a successful grape tree; smaller seeds are more likely to produce an immature or weak fruit, and can also be susceptible to disease if grown in cool climates (like the Pacific Northwest). The purpose of the course is to give an introduction into basic physical-chemical water analysis. The contents of the course are designed to give the students the following: •	An understanding and hands-on experience with modern and common analytical techniques used in water- and waste water analysis. We have recently been told that more than 300,000 women in the US will be diagnosed with breast cancer this year.
More than 40,000 women will die from breast cancer this year alone.
More than 2 million women have been diagnosed with breast cancer in their lifetime.
We may never have a cure for cancer...but you can prevent it from becoming a life-threatening problem to someone you care about.
Breast cancer prevention and early detection is something we CAN control.
Women over 50: Have your mammogram with your physician. If you are over 50, have a self-check monthly and call your doctor if you have concerns.
Women with a first degree relative (mother, daughter, sister) with breast or ovarian cancer should have annual mammograms beginning at age 30-35.
Women at average risk for breast cancer who wish to have a mammogram earlier should talk with their physician about risk factors and recommendations.
Women who are at high risk for breast cancer and have had an early breast cancer diagnosis should consult with their physician about options for preventative surgery or genetic testing.

A friend sent me this recipe, the author got it from a neighbor. The recipe is called "The Original" - they didn't say who made it the original, but I'm calling it mine! It's so simple to make, tastes great for breakfast, dessert or anytime and you can vary the amounts of ingredients to your own liking.
Preheat oven to 350F. Spray a 9x13" baking dish with cooking spray.
Pour a bit of milk into bowl; add both kinds of cereal, set bowl aside.
In large bowl, beat eggs with whisk. Add remaining milk, evaporated milk sugar and vanilla; stir until well combined. Pour milk mixtures over the cereal in the bowl. Stir well.
Sprinkle chocolate chips over the top evenly.
Bake for 35 - 40 minutes or until set. Cool for 30 minutes or so before serving.
Serve warm, right away, or at room temp or cold - but it's good no matter which way you eat it!

The main goal of this study is to analyse the relationship between political participation and economic participation. We explore the role of political participation on economic outcomes for two generations. We study the economic participation in terms of employment and earnings. Our first hypothesis predicts that political participation positively influences employment and earnings in the young generation. However, the second hypothesis predicts that political participation does not affect the economic outcomes of the younger generation. In our model, the effects of political activity may be different for the two generations. Our study takes into account the role of education, human capital accumulation, social networks, and socioeconomic status. We collect data on electoral participation for Bulgaria (1997-2011), Romania (2011), Hungary (1994-2014), Czech Republic (1995-2014), Slovakia (1995-2014). The data for Italy are from 2012. We use panel data analysis. We use different estimation method to verify the robustness of our results. Among others, we use the General method of Moments (GMM) and 2SLS estimation methods. The analysis of the estimation results reveals the following results. The empirical results support the second hypothesis for the whole sample of the countries.

The first of the two main sets of rules governing the operation of the MRT is referred to as Safety Rules 1.0 and 1.1.
The Safety Rules 1.0 and 1.1 have been written such that the individual, company and project must comply with the Safety Rules in order to operate on the MRT.
A project that desires to operate within this framework must be registered with MRT via the online system and adhere to the Safety Rules for the duration of the project’s license to operate.
A project shall be registered with MRT upon submission of a completed application form.
For application forms and supporting documents please visit Application & Submission process.
Upon the approval of the Safety Rules 1.0 & 1.1 a unique Safety Number will be assigned and will be issued to the individual or company for the duration of its MRT operation license.

In real life, the F-15E did just that, shooting down an S-300 while simultaneously tracking a second radar system -- only to be confronted by Russia's newest and most deadly S-400.
After Russia's invasion of Georgia, NATO officials and U.S. military chiefs were shocked at the sheer strength of the S-400 system -- and worried its existence would threaten NATO's entire air-defense infrastructure. As it turned out, NATO's greatest fear had been realized. During the September-November 2009 confrontation, the F-15E shot down a "fake" radar system set up in Georgia, but was unable to take down the actual radar system. I think one thing that has been missing in your argument so far is how we should define success.

I would define it in two ways.

1. The person should be able to support themselves and be able to live without depending on others.

2. They should be able to improve themselves.

Both of these have a very clear definition. There are objective ways to determine success level for these criteria.

For 1., look at an average salary. You need a decent chunk of change to live a decent life. If most people who try to do something are living that way, then the person attempting the thing is doing just fine.

2. Look at people learning things. We have many people learning all kinds of things. We can easily determine if the average person is still improving their knowledge.

We can also look at what they are doing now compared what they weren't doing in the past. We can determine the success of things that way fairly easily. We can also get a good idea looking at what our neighbors where before and after a certain event.

If the majority of people don't improve their situation with things, then the things aren't worth doing.

Polls: How Often Do You Clean Out Your Toothbrush?
We're all familiar with the dreaded toothbrush head. If you're like us, it's likely that the last time you replaced yours was a few months back or -- even worse -- it's been quite some time since you replaced it. If this sounds like you, don't worry, you're definitely not alone. Many of us, unfortunately, tend to forget that part.
But that can be dangerous, and it's not just your mouth you need to worry about.
Toothbrush handles contain an average of 20,000 bacteria per square inch, according to a recent study by Penn State. As scary as that sounds, the problem is even worse with the heads.
The most common strains of bacteria are associated with oral infections, such as Staphylococcus, Actinomyces and Enterococcus species.
But we're not here to stress you out -- we're here to help!
To get you back on track, here are your options.
1. Use a disposable brush.
2. Wash and sanitize the head regularly. Just fill a cup with water and put your head in it, making sure it is fully submerged. Microwave it for 20 seconds

A new, highly effective drug-free treatment for Alzheimer’s disease and other forms of dementia has been shown in a Phase 2 clinical trial to halt the progressive and deadly disease just by taking the drug orally. The therapy, called HXB2, is made of fragments of one protein (one of only six proteins needed to make life possible) that are assembled into what is a completely new protein, or antibody drug. The results of the trial, which is led by scientists at Boston Children's Hospital (BCH) and the New York/New Jersey region of a global biopharmaceutical company, will be presented today in a poster session at the Alzheimer’s Association International Conference.
The HXB2 drug was invented at BCH. The protein it targets, β-amyloid, is present in the brains of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) patients and is a key cause of its progressive loss of memory and function. The drug prevents β-amyloid from forming in the brain, and thus prevents its damage, potentially protecting and preserving the brain’s structure and function.
The team’s results show that the HXB2 drug treatment was able to stabilize and slow disease progression in AD patients by up to eight years longer than control

The most important thing, of course, is that every child receives a great education. The second most important thing is that every child receives the education they need, when they need it.
In the past, school districts and state education agencies used a single, comprehensive assessment to decide which kids were “ready for college.” That wasn’t good enough.
The old model used only a single indicator to make a decision that affects a child’s life for life. The new approach focuses on multiple indicators, to ensure that children receive the education they need, when they need it.
The new standards are being adopted across the country. They replace the old, often irrelevant, multiple-choice tests with more robust tests, that reflect the curriculum, and more challenging assignments that help students reach their full potential.
The transition to Common Core is in full swing, and the results are already showing. In New York, students are getting the standardized test scores they need to get into college. In New York State, students are getting the high school graduation rates they need to get into college.
The common core is a big deal because the curriculum is the most important element of a student’s education. If you have a good curriculum, you have a high-quality education

A recent survey of Canadian teachers found that more than 70 per cent believe that their mental health is negatively impacted by their work. The survey, which included 2,000 high school teachers and 1,200 elementary school teachers, found that teachers who said that their mental health had been affected were significantly more likely to say they would feel emotionally drained each week or feel stressed by the work.
Teacher stress has also been linked to negative student outcomes. A recent study published in the New York Times found that children who were exposed to high levels of teacher stress were more likely to experience “poor academic outcomes including poor grades and lower levels of academic achievement.” This is not at all surprising considering that students are taught by teachers who themselves may be struggling with stress to meet deadlines and stay on top of things.
There are many ways teachers can manage their own stress and mental health as they navigate the COVID-19 pandemic and its impacts; one of them is yoga. The Summit, which takes place at the University of Colorado at Boulder School of Journalism and Mass Communication, will bring together more than 120 leaders in technology, journalism, government, education and policy from the U.S., Canada and Europe.
Jason Fung also serves as the Communications Officer at the Canadian Embassy to the United States, and is responsible for developing and implementing a comprehensive Government Communications Strategy.
Fung is the former Director of Strategic Communications at the City of Vancouver. While there he was the lead architect for a number of initiatives that have become international best practice models in media relations. He was also a co-investigator with his colleagues at the University of British Columbia on “The Media and Democracy Project,” and was directly responsible for the development of the national and regional media relations strategy for the 2010 Olympic Games.

After ten years of marriage, four years of separation, and months of fighting, we were at a breaking point.
Two weeks ago, I picked up my dogs and drove to my parents’ house with the goal of figuring out how I’d move forward for my children’s sake.
I knew something was off in our marriage when his mother passed away nine years ago. His emotional response was very different than mine; he was sad but also angry. We’d both loved his mother deeply, and at the same time, it seemed he wasn’t grieving. When our son was born, that pattern continued.

I have created an interactive map of the different stages of alcohol and drug use with my fellow Narcotics Anonymous (NA) members. When you click on the stage of your life, it will take you to an article that explains a little bit about what it means to be at that particular stage, or it will lead to a post within this blog.
I am using this map to help me understand the stages of alcohol, drugs and the disease of addiction and to provide readers with information about the different phases of active addiction.
I am not saying this is 100% how we all operate; my hope is that by putting thoughts to an image on the internet where we can access it, I am hoping to inspire more thoughtful discourse.
This idea came to fruition while I was walking, thinking about how to explain to a woman that I work with about the stages of addiction when she asked why she doesn’t get as far along in NA as the rest of her group. They have gotten clean for various lengths of time, and she feels like she has “stagnated” or gotten stuck in the addiction. The GLOBE programme, led by the NERC Centre for Environment, Fishery and Aquaculture Science (Cefas), is a marine observation and data programme that has been running since 1995. Its aim is to promote international cooperation through scientific observation to promote sustainable use of ocean biological resources and the protection of the marine environment. With the increase in the use of drones in the marine environment in recent years, the Cefas Marine Observatories Network (CMON) has identified a need for a standardised and automated approach to the collection of real-time imagery from a range of different platforms. This standardisation will also allow different sensors to be easily integrated into the existing infrastructure.
The main objective of the GLOBE project funded by the European Union (EU) Horizon2020 Programme, is to develop a ‘Data Delivery and Data Acquisition’ (DDA) platform, an IoT tool that uses standard interfaces to automatically transmit data from remotely located autonomous platforms such as drones (e.g. UAVs, UUVs, AUVs) and in-situ sensors, with the aim to collect more accurate data in a timely and cost-effective manner. In this article, Sander van der Linden from the University of Cambridge's Social Psychology Centre, outlines how our understanding of social isolation can be used to help us understand loneliness.
As an example, he uses his own research into loneliness at Cambridge University, and his recent study into social isolatoin during the COVID-19 pandemic.
The relationship between our experiences of loneliness and those of other people is not well understood.
In our own research, we tried something new to see if we could explain the relationship better. We asked participants to do a series of tasks that could reveal how the experience of loneliness in our lives depends on the experience of loneliness of others.
In these tasks, we asked whether it might be possible to make sense of why people feel the way they do about being lonely by comparing their loneliness to others'.
So far, the answer, which we have found both in our own work, and in that of other researchers, is a qualified yes. There is some evidence that we feel more lonely when we are in a worse social position than others.

They are usually just being pretentious when they use this term, but this isn't about a person who is being intentionally obtuse or trying to be edgy. On top of that, even though the first season is the most linear, it has some of the most memorable moments.

The first week of February I participated in the 5 week "Make Art That Sells" Bootcamp with Kassia Meadowcraft. The first week was a deep dive into value, and I chose a subject that is new to me - flowers of all shapes and sizes. This painting has been through all the steps of the Bootcamp, and has been a joy to work through.
This is the third watercolour painting I've made that was inspired by this beautiful pink tree. This time I started first with a sketch and then watercolour, and it's the first one that I like best.
This was such a fun painting to work with, I just love to mix and match the fun patterns. I had a wonderful time finding bits of patterns - the pink and green flowers are in the fabric, the peach and orange ones came from an embroidery hoop.
My friend and fellow artist Kassia Meadowcraft wrote the book "Make Art That Sells" which I have been recommending to anyone who wants information on how to better market their art and connect with others. She is a wonderful artist, teacher and person.

This is not about me, as usual, but some kids I went to school with some years back.

They didn't really do anything, but their parents certainly were not the nicest people in the world, especially the mother. We had all become young adults (some had left school around the time of this, I'm only 21) and we started meeting up for stuff. One of their parties was one where I brought my girlfriend over since we were seeing each other at school, she happened to get her head through someone's door with a guy on top of her and she immediately turned to me. I told her to get up and out quick and we bailed as quick as possible.

It turned out that their dad had been a local police officer. I didn't realise that until after.

They were all the same age as me and they'd grown up fast.

Dr. Ngozichukwu Obiligbo is a practicing medical doctor and health educator in the UK. He is the founder and CEO of Health Awareness Foundation and a member of The Nigeria Association of Pharmaceutical Practitioners of England and Wales.
What are drugs and drug addiction?
Drugs refer to substances of biological or chemical origin with the potential to alter the behaviour or function of an organism, either by producing a psychoactive effect. As in the case of opioids, the substances act on or mimic the same brain chemicals as natural painkillers (endorphin and adrenalin). Drugs in this category can be prescription drugs or street drugs.
Opioids or Opioid-Based Substances include oxycodone, morphine, and heroin. Opioids affect the reward pathways of your brain, by producing euphoria and a heightened sensation of pain relief. Opioids are highly addictive. This is because they directly activate opioid receptors in your brain.

I was lucky to have such a good friend living there. She gave me some good insights into the French way as she lives there (and she says she loves it!) and I gave her some of my US-ness. We had a lot of fun!
My host mom, who is French but originally from Algeria, wanted me to do a French class with her. The other woman is also from Algeria but is living in France right now. The teacher was from Haiti and so was an interesting mix!

There are many types of people who have a hard time making friends, and many causes of anxiety-based social difficulties. For the sake of this article, we will be discussing one subset of shyness-related social difficulties: interpersonal shyness. People who suffer from this form are usually more socially active by themselves, and would not consider themselves to have a social phobia that is as intense or severe. But they will find that when they are in social situations there they are extremely uncomfortable as their fear of being rejected dominates their thoughts and thoughts of social situations.
The causes of interpersonal shyness come in many different formats, and often are not a single, obvious reason for why an individual might develop their issues. Interpersonal shyness can be thought of as an anxiety disorder in that it is a fear and/or anxiety that an individual experiences in social situations. Unlike anxiety brought on by a generalized fear of all situations, interpersonal shyness is often linked to particular social contexts that you are likely to find stressful. The most common example that most people find is speaking to a large group of people. Some people feel shy even in small groups, while others might feel shy only when talking to their opposite gender group.

One of the best things about being an entrepreneur is that you get to dictate what you do. But one of the downsides is you have to say yes to everything (at least at first).
I’ve always enjoyed building something from scratch, and this is my third company. My first company was a video production company that I set up in my college apartment when I was 20. It went pretty well, and made almost $1m. Then I took it public and it was listed on the New York Stock Exchange — something that I had always dreamed about. That was the most fun. But it’s too easy to lose momentum when you’re part of a group.
A lot of people come and go, and you need to deal with a lot of bureaucracy. That’s something I don’t really care to deal with. It’s been nice to rekindle my love for just doing things on my own again, having more control, and working with better people.

It's not that the world would be a better place if everyone acted as if they were Jewish...

It's that, in this particular circumstance, the fact that the *Jew* was able to escape to a place of relative safety and safety from the Nazis without a struggle would have made the rest of the Jewish population very grateful. And, while they would not like to admit this, by choosing to act as if he was not Jewish they *were* making his choice to escape and find a new life for himself harder.

It's also worth noting that Hitler and the Nazis were willing to kill *Jews* to get what they wanted - they were not above it. It's that they were a bit more restrained than we are led to believe, and that they were trying to find new ways to exterminate the Jews and other minorities.

To put it simply... the Nazis were more than just cruel. They were also intelligent, calculating, and *evil*.

The first step in the process of getting a child ready for the school curriculum is to assess their ability in numeracy and literacy, and also determine how much support they need in both their numeracy and literacy learning.
The assessment process consists of 2 elements; formal diagnostic testing during the school term and feedback sessions between parent and teacher during the second week of the new school term.
The tests give the teacher a good indication as to the individual child’s strengths, weaknesses, needs and expectations for the upcoming year.
The process provides parents with feedback during the second week of the new term to assist them in developing or extending their child’s learning.
How can you tell when your child may need support?
The child may be very confident writing stories at home and may have difficulty reading the words they have just written.
The child is unable to follow verbal instructions.
The child may have difficulty completing tasks or activities that involve many components.
The child does not like to read and refuses to practise.
The child reads at a very basic level and requires support to develop more advanced comprehension and language skills.
The child gets very upset and refuses to follow written instructions.
The child may become overwhelmed by visual stimuli and experience difficulty coping with tasks

What is a BPO call centre?
What is a BPO call centre service?
A BPO call centre is a type of customer relationship management function to enhance the overall productivity of any company or enterprise. Its purpose is to effectively respond to the customer’s queries and complaints over the phone in an effective manner. The BPO call centre employs experienced customer executives to ensure that issues are resolved immediately. When the customer feels valued and the matter is resolved effectively, the company wins the customer’s loyalty for life. All BPO call centre customers are also provided with an option to log a complaint via e-mail. This way, the customers can be reached even after the call is terminated.
Why is the BPO call centre more efficient than human operators?
It is often said that the time taken to address the customer problem is a major difference between the BPO call centre and human operators. This is completely not true. The time taken to address the customer queries depends upon a few pre-determined factors. A BPO call centre, therefore, is much more efficient and productive than humans. This is due to the following reasons.

On February 15, the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) issued its first of two reports examining current and projected food prices. In a letter from Agriculture Secretary Sonny Perdue to House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-CA), Perdue stated, “Prices for the items we buy have spiked sharply, and for all but the biggest buyers, we’re paying more for everything from meat to milk, eggs to fruits and vegetable — and, yes, that includes coffee.” The agency predicts that overall annual retail food inflation will average 2.7% during the years 2019 to 2028. This is substantially higher than average annual growth rates of 18.6% for 2008 to 2018, 3% for 2006 to 2007, 4.7% for 2011 to 2012, and 2.5% for 2013 to 2014. The report also concludes that the national retail costs of food is expected to decrease over one and ten year averages for a wide range of major categories to as little as -2.6%, indicating a significant downward trend in total costs of food over the next several years.
This post was published on February 18, 2020 . It was updated on March 1, 2020 by Government Executive.

Battle of the Sexes is an inspiring look back to 1973, when tennis champ Billie Jean King played a match against golf champion Bobby Riggs, who would go on to call himself the “Pigeon King of Queens” and claim he’s the best to play the sport. At the time, King was ranked No. 2 in the world; she was the top female player ever to appear on the male tour, and, despite her obvious prowess, she wasn’t paid as much as her male colleagues. The two met in the “Battle of the Sexes” at Chastain Memorial Park, a tennis club in Atlanta, to settle who was the better player. It was clear who was better right away, but the match also sparked a cultural awakening among women and women’s rights, sparking the feminist movement. It’s an inspiring match that really shows how far women’s tennis has come.
I had never heard of this match, though it is obviously a huge deal and a pivotal moment in sports history. It’s amazing how just a match can spark a culture shift that affects us all.

In the fall of 1990, at a time when no other professional league had been in existence longer in the United States than the NFL, the XFL made its debut. It was an unusual venture; one that was not necessarily a direct offshoot of a well-established and successful existing league, but rather, a “startup” venture whose sole owner was Warner Communications Chairman and CEO, Ted Turner. The XFL was billed as “American football for the modern era”, and was a high-action, innovative, and entertaining brand of football. It featured an aggressive play-calling approach that included a number of exotic, unconventional plays, and was promoted to be a “fast, fun, innovative, and exciting game that puts fans in the game”.
On Saturday, February 9, 2002, Turner announced plans to put a total of six teams in operation for the following spring. The original eight XFL teams were the Miami Methuselahs, New York/New Jersey Metrostars, Orlando Predators, San Francisco Demons, Seattle Sounders, St. Louis Storm, Tampa Bay Bloodhounds, and Washington Enemy. For the benefit of the uninitiated, we're currently in the early stages of another war against ISIS, which is why you've seen the "IS" letters and numbers (ISIS, ISIL, Daesh, etc.) thrown around so much since the election. 

Anyway, the goal of these new air campaigns is not to take out ISIS, or even remove their leaders from power. That's not entirely impossible, but that would be extremely difficult at this point. 

The goal is to put so much of a hurt on its fighters that they can no longer launch successful strikes against civilian populations. And they know that our air attacks are hitting the wrong targets quite frequently. So the next best step was to hit the wrong targets in the least offensive way possible--the civilian populations of other countries, which are largely uninvolved in this conflict. 

For the vast majority of us, none of this is personal anyway. And it's not personal for them either. This is all a political game, with political goals.
